name: mpd-inspector

metadata.name: mpd-inspector
metadata.summary: A module to parse, analyze and validate MPEG-DASH MPD

documentation:
# mpd-inspector

## Overview
This is a module to parse MPEG-DASH Media Presentation Documents (aka. MPD) from files or URLs, and interpret them
This package is built as a fork from the excellent [mpd-parser](https://github.com/avishaycohen/mpd-parser/tree/main). The main reasons for forking it are:
1. I needed to change the behaviour to be closer to the MPEG-DASH spec, in particular DASH-IF IOP rules.
1. I wanted to parse all MPD attributes into native Python types (including for datetimes and durations)
2. I wanted to be able to expose unparsed elements
3. I wanted to add a layer of inspection/analysis to make it possible to calculate implicit/derived values (eg. start and duration of periods in multi-period MPD) - in particular again for validation against the DASH-IF Interoperability guidance


## Installation
```shell
$ python -m pip install mpd-inspector
```

## Usage
### Importing

```python
from mpd_parser.parser import Parser
```
### parse from string
```python
with open("path/to/file.mpd", mode="r") as manifest_file:
    mpd_string = manifest_file.read()
    parsed_mpd = Parser.from_string(mpd_string)
```

### parse from file
```python
input_file = "path/to/file.mpd"
mpd = Parser.from_file(input_file)
```

### parse from url
```python
input_url = "https://my-server.com/path/to/stream.mpd"
mpd = Parser.from_url(input_url)
```

### inspect it
```python
mpd_inspector = MPDInspector(mpd)
```

### convert back to string
```python
mpd_as_xml_string = Parser.to_string(parsed_mpd)
```

## Example manifests
Taken from a variety of places

### Build locally
```shell
poetry build
```

modules.name: mpd_inspector.value_statements
documentation: class AnyTypeMeta(type):
def __instancecheck__(cls, instance):
print("type " + type(instance).__name__)
if type(instance) is StatedValue:
return isinstance(instance.value, cls)
return super().__instancecheck__(instance)
class StatedValue(metaclass=AnyTypeMeta):

methods.name: mpd_inspector.parser.parser.MPDParser.from_string
documentation:
generate a parsed mpd object from a given string

Args:
    manifest_as_string (str): string repr of a manifest file.

Returns:
    an object representing the MPD tag and all it's XML goodies

methods.name: mpd_inspector.parser.parser.MPDParser.from_file
documentation:
Generate a parsed mpd object from a given file name
Args:
    manifest_file_name (str): file name to parse

Returns:
    an object representing the MPD tag and all it's XML goodies

methods.name: mpd_inspector.parser.parser.MPDParser.from_url
documentation:
Generate a parsed mpd object from a given URL
Args:
    url (str): the url of the file to parse

Returns:
    an object representing the MPD tag and all it's XML goodies

methods.name: mpd_inspector.parser.parser.MPDParser.to_string
documentation:
generate a string xml from a given MPD tag object

Args:
        mpd: MPD object created by one of the parser factories
Returns:
        a string representation of the MPD object, xml formatted dash mpeg manifest

Parser class, holds factories to work with manifest files.
can parse:
1. from string - using the from_string method
2. from file - TBA
3. from url - TBA

methods.name: mpd_inspector.inspector.RepresentationInspector.segment_information
documentation:
Return the element that defines the way to access the media segments.
The term 'Segment Information' is taken from ISO/IEC 23009-1, 5.3.9.2.1

classes.name: mpd_inspector.parser.mpd_tags.MPD
documentation:
MPD class represents the root of a mpd manifest,
it is the top most tag of an XML file following the DASH format

the element passed for MPD should be the root of the lxml.etree
